skill.name: youtube-transcript
skill.description: Extract, clean, and summarize YouTube transcripts using yt-dlp, subtitle files, and optional Whisper fallback.
skill.summary: Extract YouTube subtitles and turn them into clean transcripts, summaries, chapters, and keyword notes.
skill.author: Doyajin174

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly detected many shell command examples and one network URL example in SKILL.md. These commands match the transcript extraction purpose, but they still create medium risk because the workflow can install tools, fetch remote media, and write local transcript or audio files. No evidence found of credential theft, obfuscation, prompt injection, or malicious persistence.

medium_findings.title: External Command Workflow Requires User URL Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs use of yt-dlp, sed, and Whisper commands for subtitle listing, subtitle download, text conversion, audio extraction, and transcription. This is expected for a YouTube transcript skill, but agents must treat video URLs as untrusted input and avoid unsafe shell interpolation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed commands are direct evidence of external process execution. The examples quote VIDEO_URL placeholders, so the risk is operational rather than clear command injection.

medium_findings.title: Package Installation Guidance Includes Privileged Commands
medium_findings.description: The requirements section recommends installing yt-dlp with brew, apt, or pip, including a sudo apt command. This is a legitimate setup instruction, but privileged package installation increases host risk if executed without user approval.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 32 contains a sudo package installation command inside setup documentation. It is clearly present, but it is not evidence of malicious intent by itself.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded YouTube URL Example
low_findings.description: The examples include a YouTube URL placeholder to show the expected user request format. This indicates intended network access to YouTube content, not an exfiltration endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears in a user-facing example and points to YouTube. No evidence found that data is sent to an unrelated service.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Finding Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at the skill description line, but the file does not describe cryptographic operations there. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, insecure cipher use, or password hashing logic in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is frontmatter text describing transcript download behavior. The semantic context does not support a cryptography concern.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Finding Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance at the invalid URL troubleshooting row. The line only tells the user to verify URL format and does not collect host, network, user, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is an error handling table entry about URL validation. No reconnaissance command or host inspection behavior is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains shell command examples for installing tools, listing subtitles, downloading subtitles, converting VTT text, extracting audio, and running Whisper. These are functional requirements for the skill but must be run with normal command safety controls.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Multiple fenced command blocks contain executable shell commands. The commands serve the stated purpose and do not show hidden malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Video content is hard to review when captions are scattered or unavailable. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through subtitle extraction, cleanup, and summarization workflows.
actual_capabilities: Checks available subtitle languages with yt-dlp before downloading.
Downloads manual subtitles first and falls back to auto-generated subtitles.
Converts VTT subtitle files into cleaner plain text transcripts.
Removes duplicate caption lines from generated subtitle output.
Supports summary, chapter outline, and keyword extraction from transcripts.
Suggests Whisper transcription when no subtitles are available and user approval is given.
limitations: Requires yt-dlp to be installed before transcript extraction can run.
Cannot access private, deleted, region-blocked, or unavailable videos without proper user access.
Transcript quality depends on the source captions or automatic speech recognition quality.
Whisper fallback can require large downloads, processing time, and local storage.

use_cases.title: Lecture Review
use_cases.description: Students can turn long lecture videos into cleaned transcripts, summaries, and chapter notes.

use_cases.title: Research Capture
use_cases.description: Researchers can extract multilingual captions and compare key points across video sources.

use_cases.title: Content Repurposing
use_cases.description: Creators can convert video captions into drafts for notes, posts, or documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Transcript
prompt_templates.scenario: Download a clean transcript from one public video.
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the transcript from this YouTube video and save a clean text version: [video URL]

prompt_templates.title: Language Selection
prompt_templates.scenario: Check captions and choose a specific subtitle language.
prompt_templates.prompt: List the available subtitles for this video, then extract the Korean transcript if it exists: [video URL]

prompt_templates.title: Summary and Chapters
prompt_templates.scenario: Create structured notes from a transcript.
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the transcript for this video, remove duplicate caption lines, then provide a summary and chapter outline: [video URL]

prompt_templates.title: Fallback Transcription Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Handle a video with no available captions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether this video has captions. If it does not, estimate the audio download size and ask before using Whisper: [video URL]

output_examples.input: Extract the transcript from a public tutorial video.
output_examples.output: A plain text transcript with repeated caption fragments removed and readable paragraph breaks.

output_examples.input: Summarize a lecture after extracting captions.
Get English and Korean subtitles for one video.
output_examples.output: Separate cleaned transcript files for each available language, with a note if one language is missing.

best_practices: Confirm that the video is public and that transcript use follows platform and content owner rules.
List available subtitles before downloading so the best manual caption track is used first.
Ask before downloading audio or running Whisper because this can use bandwidth and storage.
anti_patterns: Do not run package installation commands with elevated privileges without explicit user approval.
Do not paste untrusted video URLs into shell commands without safe argument handling.
Do not rely on auto-generated captions as exact quotations without checking the source.

faq.question: Does this skill download the full video?
faq.answer: It normally skips video downloads and retrieves subtitles only. Audio download is suggested only for Whisper fallback.

faq.question: Can it handle auto-generated captions?
faq.answer: Yes. It tries manual subtitles first, then uses auto-generated subtitles when needed.

faq.question: Can it extract subtitles in more than one language?
faq.answer: Yes. It can list available subtitle languages and download selected language tracks.

faq.question: What happens if a video has no captions?
faq.answer: The skill suggests checking file size and asking before using Whisper to transcribe downloaded audio.

faq.question: Does this skill require network access?
faq.answer: Yes. yt-dlp needs network access to check and download YouTube subtitle data.

faq.question: Is the transcript always accurate?
faq.answer: No. Accuracy depends on the available subtitle track or speech recognition output.
